pi_items.source: All three fonts are used in setup_task.sh (lines 29, 39, 65) and verifier.py line 73 matches exactly
Task description specifies 'Arial, 16pt, Bold, Dark Blue'. Verifier accepts Arial or Liberation Sans, but metadata claim of Arial is correct per description.
Task description and verifier.py line 107 both check for exactly 16.0pt
Task description specifies 'Bold', verifier.py line 120 checks h1_style.font.bold
Hex 00008B converts to RGB(0,0,139) = Dark Blue. Verified with hex conversion (00=0, 00=0, 8B=139). Task description specifies 'Dark Blue', verifier.py lines 24-29 accept this color range.
Task description specifies 'Verdana', verifier.py line 149 checks body_style.font.name == 'Verdana'
Task description specifies '10pt', verifier.py line 155 checks for 10.0pt
Task description specifies 'Justified alignment', verifier.py line 162 checks == WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.JUSTIFY

CONTRADICTION: Metadata uses key 'Body', but task description says 'Text Body (or Normal)', and verifier.py line 129 explicitly checks ['Text Body', 'Normal']. Default python-docx style is 'Normal', not 'Body'. The verifier does NOT check for a style named 'Body'.
UNVERIFIED/INCOMPLETE: Task description specifies '1.15 line spacing', verifier.py lines 169-173 check for 1.1 <= line_spacing <= 1.2, but metadata does not include line_spacing field at all.

privileged_info_summary: Task involves cleaning a synthetically-generated messy Word document with direct formatting violations. Draft document path is /home/ga/Documents/remote_work_policy_draft.docx, output path is /home/ga/Documents/remote_work_policy_clean.docx. Document contains forbidden fonts (Comic Sans MS, Courier New, Liberation Mono) that must be removed. Heading 1 style must be Arial 16pt Bold with Dark Blue color (hex 00008B = RGB 0,0,139). Body text style requires Verdana 10pt with justified alignment and 1.15 line spacing (note: metadata incorrectly labels style as 'Body' when it should be 'Normal' or 'Text Body'). Data is synthetic, generated by Python docx library during task setup.
